questions.question: What are the key differences between the NIST Cybersecurity Framework and ISO 27001?
keyPoints: NIST CSF is voluntary and risk-based, while ISO 27001 is certifiable international standard
NIST has 5 core functions, ISO uses PDCA cycle
NIST is more flexible, ISO has stricter documentation requirements
Different origins: NIST (US government), ISO (international standards body)

questions.question: Describe the four-phase process for implementing a security framework in an organization.
keyPoints: Phase 1: Preparation and Assessment - scope definition, current state analysis, gap assessment
Phase 2: Planning and Design - develop implementation plan, customize framework, establish metrics
Phase 3: Implementation - deploy controls, integrate with processes, train staff
Phase 4: Operation and Maintenance - monitor effectiveness, conduct audits, continuous improvement
Emphasis on executive sponsorship and stakeholder engagement throughout process
